chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No.D-2977 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Fresh case 1. For order on Misc. No.13141/2025 (Urgent/App). 2. For order on office objection No.1 to 6. 3. For order on Misc. No.13142/2025 (Exemption/App) 4. For order on Misc. No.13143/2025 (Stay/App). 5. For hearing of main case..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 01.07.2025 Mr. Nadeem Memon, Advocate for the petitioner..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. 1. Through instant petition, the petitioner has challenged the order dated 25.06.2025 issued by the Senior Superintendent of Police, District East Karachi, [Respondent No.4] to investigate the cause of death of the deceased Mst. Jameela @ Mobeena wife of Rasool Bux, daughter of Arbab Ali Memon. 2. The counsel for the petitioner was put on notice to satisfy as to how this petition was maintainable against the impugned order dated 25.06.2025 whereby a committee comprising of the senior police officials was constituted to investigate into the cause of death of the deceased wife of the petitioner. 3. Learned counsel for the petitioner contended that the petition was maintainable, as the wife of the petitioner was suffering from chronic diseases, she remained under constant care and she died under mysterious conditions. The police intend to investigate into the cause of her death without any lawful authority. 4. Heard learned counsel perused material on record. 5. Perusal of the record revealed that Mst. Jameela @ Mobeena wife of the petitioner died on 26.05.2025, as per the death certificate issued by the Dow University Ojha Campus, the case of deceased was suspect of poisoning and that could be suicidal or homicidal.

Perusal of the record revealed that Mst. Jameela @ Mobeena wife of the petitioner died on 26.05.2025, as per the death certificate issued by the Dow University Ojha Campus, the case of deceased was suspect of poisoning and that could be suicidal or homicidal. She was referred for postmortem to Jinnah Post Graduate Medical Centre, Karachi. 6. The sister of the deceased namely Rubina moved an application to the Deputy Inspector General of Police, East Karachi and 2 requested for registration of an FIR of the incident by leveling specific allegation against the petitioner Rasool Bux, his nephew Gulzar Ahmed and Mst. Rizwana wife of Gulzar Ahmed for committing murder of the deceased. The application of Mst. Rubina (available at page-45 of the memo of the petition) was forwarded to SSP East, who constituted a committee headed by SDPO Shahra-e-Faisal, SDPO Mahmoodabad, SDPO Sohrab Goth, SHO Ferozabad and SHO Sachal as members with terms of reference to investigate the cause of death of the deceased Mst. Jameela @ Mobeena and furnish its recommendations. 7. Since the impugned order was issued by the SSP East, Karachi to investigate the cause of death of the deceased Mst. Jameela @ Mobeena on the application of her sister, which apparently did not suffer from any illegality, infirmity or perversity, calling for interference by this Court. It is the duty of the police to investigate every incident in accordance with law and if from material available it transpired that an offence of cognizable nature was committed by any person, appropriate action by recording a complaint under Section- 154 Cr.P.C should follow. 8. Petitioner has failed to point out as to how investigation into the cause of death of Mst. Jameela @ Mobeena would result in impinging his fundamental rights warranting interference of this Court. No case for the indulgence under the writ jurisdiction of this Court is made out.

Jameela @ Mobeena would result in impinging his fundamental rights warranting interference of this Court. No case for the indulgence under the writ jurisdiction of this Court is made out. The petition being devoid of merits is dismissed in limine along with pending applications. JUDGE JUDGE Ayaz Gul
